Submissions are open on the 1st and 15th of the month.

For when you want answers but don’t want to ask it yourself.

Polls sometimes include adult and/or sensitive topics. While we make an effort to tag common triggers appropriately, some polls may be heavy or NSFW.
blog.name: incognitopolls

tags: I have read incognitopolls' submission guidelines
submission_terms.title: Submit a poll
submission_terms.guidelines:
Submissions are accepted on the 1st and 15th of the month. If it is not the 1st or 15th, your poll submission will be discarded.

Check the pinned post for more details on submission guidelines.

Include (1) a title, and (2) all of the answer choices you want the poll to list, up to a maximum of 12.

posts.summary:
#1: A house with 3 floors including basement. There's a basement, a floor at ground level, and one floor above ground level.
#2:...

content.text: #1: A house with 3 floors including basement. There's a basement, a floor at ground level, and one floor above ground level.
#2: A house with 3 floors and NO basement. There's one floor at ground level, and two floors above ground level. There is nothing below ground level.

content.question: In your opinion, is a house with 3 floors (including a basement) a mansion? What about a house with 3 floors but no basement?

answers.answer_text: Both of these are mansions
The FIRST house (3 floors including basement) is a mansion; the second is not
The SECOND house (3 floors, no basement) is a mansion; the first is not
Neither of these are mansions
Whether it's a mansion or not depends on something else

posts.summary:
Have you ever had a medical or dental procedure involving nitrous oxide ("laughing gas")? Where are you from?
Yes, North...

content.question: Have you ever had a medical or dental procedure involving nitrous oxide ("laughing gas")? Where are you from?

answers.answer_text: Yes, North America
Yes, South America
Yes, Oceania
Yes, Asia
Yes, Europe
Yes, Africa
No, North America
No, South America
No, Oceania
No, Asia
No, Europe
No, Africa

content.text: If the procedure that involved nitrous oxide was in a different part of the world from where you live, vote for where the procedure took place.

Because this comes up on every poll involving continents: yes, there are several different ways to delineate the Americas. Yes, there is debate as to whether Central America is part of North America, should be grouped with South America to draw a distinction from the US and Canada, should be considered an entirely separate entity, etc. Tumblr polls only allow for 12 options which means it can't be its own category. If you're from Central America, you may vote for whichever you feel better represents how you would categorize it.

posts.summary:
If you (1) have a fetish (sexual or not) and (2) make fanworks, do you involve your fetish in those fanworks?
Yes, I make...

content.question: If you (1) have a fetish (sexual or not) and (2) make fanworks, do you involve your fetish in those fanworks?

answers.answer_text: Yes, I make fanworks involving my fetish(es) in every fandom I'm in
Yes, but it varies with the source media and/or character's vibes
Yes, and I'm only into fandoms that are compatible with my fetish(es)
No, I'm too self-conscious to make anything that involves my fetish
No, I'm actively against making fanworks that include my fetish
No, I'm just not interested in combining these things
Other
I make fanworks but don't have a fetish
I have a fetish but don't make fanworks
I don't do either off these things/show results

content.text: For the purpose of this poll, "making fanworks" includes if you specifically have someone else make the work for you– e.g. commissioning works with particular themes or noting particular themes that you like in a swap/exchange/etc.

answers.answer_text: Everyone in my life knows my deadname
ALMOST everyone in my life knows my deadname, but not all
Most people in my life know my deadname
About half of the people in my life know my deadname
Some people in my life know my deadname
Very few people in my life know my deadname
I can think of exactly one person in my life currently who knows my deadname
Nobody in my life now knows my deadname
Other/it's complicated
I'm trans and still use my given name in some or all contexts, show results
Not trans, show results

content.text: "People in your life" meaning people you interact with regularly and have some sort of relationship with– friends, partners, family, coworkers, classmates, etc. Don't count, for example, strangers on the bus, or the cashier at the grocery store, and don't count people you no longer have a relationship with.

posts.summary: This is asking about casual references to Harry Potter being applied to things that aren't about Harry Potter. For example,...

content.text: This is asking about casual references to Harry Potter being applied to things that aren't about Harry Potter. For example, calling someone "he who must not be named," using "patronus" in the same way as (the slang/non-native use of) "spirit animal," etc.

content.question: Does it bother you when people reference Harry Potter in casual language/memes/etc, even if the person making the reference doesn't support JKR or engage with Harry Potter otherwise?

answers.answer_text: Yes, I hate it. If you "don't support" JKR, stop giving HP cultural relevance.
Yes, I dislike it but you can't erase a pop culture touchstone overnight
I dislike JKR but offhand references are harmless/not the same as supporting it
I dislike JKR; it's wrong or tone-deaf in some contexts but neutral/fine in some
I dislike JKR and HP but I actually think casual references are good
I actively like JKR and/or HP
Other opinion
No opinion/show results

posts.summary: "Store brand" means a product that is produced for and sold by a specific grocery store. "Name brand" products are sold at...

content.text: "Store brand" means a product that is produced for and sold by a specific grocery store. "Name brand" products are sold at multiple retailers under a recognizable brand name. For example, Coca-Cola is a name brand; Big K Cola is Kroger's store brand.
This is asking specifically about groceries, not other goods.

content.question: How many of the groceries used in your household are store brand, as opposed to name brand?

answers.answer_text: Exclusively store brand, I/we NEVER buy name brand
Mostly store brand, some/sometimes name brand
About 50/50
Mostly name brand, some/sometimes store brand
Exclusively name brand, I/we NEVER buy store brand
I'm not sure
This question doesn't apply to my life

posts.summary: "Other young relatives" means non-sibling relatives around your age that you grew up with; for example, cousins that lived...

content.text: "Other young relatives" means non-sibling relatives around your age that you grew up with; for example, cousins that lived nearby and you spent a lot of time with. Don't count (for example) cousins that live in another state that you saw once a year.

content.question: Do you have siblings? Did you take baths together as young children? Do you think it's normal/fine for young children to take baths together?

answers.answer_text: I have siblings; we took baths together; I think this is normal
I have siblings; we took baths together, but I DON'T think this is normal
I have siblings; we did NOT take baths together, but I think doing so is normal
I have siblings; we did NOT take baths together; I DON'T think it's normal
No siblings, but other young relatives; we took baths together; normal
No siblings, but other young relatives; we took baths together, but NOT normal
No siblings, but other young relatives; DIDN'T take baths together; normal
No siblings, but other young relatives; DIDN'T take baths together; NOT normal
I don't have anyone with whom this would apply, but I think it's normal
I don't have anyone w/ whom this would apply, but I think it's NOT normal
Other

content.text: Anon grew up in a large, poor family and there were often up to 4 kids in the bath at the same time when they were little. Anon wonders if this is a common experience or if their perspective is skewed because of their particular background.
Define "young children" in whatever way makes sense to you.
